differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis with portal cavernoma
Mesenteric ischemia (due to shunting or portomesenteric thrombosis)
Hepatocellular carcinoma in the setting of portal vein thrombosis
next_steps: CT angiography of the abdomen to evaluate for mesenteric ischemia or acute thrombus extension
Transjugular liver biopsy with hepatic vein pressure gradient (HVPG) measurement
Hematologic evaluation for underlying thrombophilia including JAK2 mutation and PNH screening
Enteroscopy or CTA to rule out chronic mesenteric ischemia
Nutritional assessment and management for weight loss and postprandial pain

differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis with portal cavernoma and chronic mesenteric venous ischemia
Non-cirrhotic portal hypertension related to trauma-induced portal vein injury
Occult pancreatic or duodenal malignancy causing secondary venous compression
next_steps: CT angiography of the abdomen (pancreatic/mesenteric protocol) to evaluate vascular anatomy and rule out occult malignancy
Transjugular liver biopsy and hepatic venous pressure gradient measurement to distinguish pre-sinusoidal vs. sinusoidal hypertension
Complete hematologic workup for prothrombotic states (JAK2, PNH, protein C/S, antithrombin, factor V Leiden)
Serologic testing for Schistosoma and stool examination for ova and parasites
Echocardiogram to exclude cardiac causes of congestion
Nutrition and gastroenterology consultation for management of postprandial ischemia and nutritional optimization
